neighborhoods.name: Waterford City Centre (Viking Triangle)
neighborhoods.description: The historic heart of Ireland's oldest city, centred on the Viking Triangle with medieval museums, Reginald's Tower, and a compact walkable core along the River Suir
neighborhoods.character: Urban historic, walkable, culturally rich with Viking and medieval heritage

housing.predominantType: Apartments and terraced houses in city centre, modern developments along the quays

neighborhoods.character_notes: Viking Triangle museums, Waterford Crystal visitor centre nearby. Weekly farmers market. Theatre Royal. Vibrant pub scene on John Street and The Quay. Bus Éireann hub.

neighborhoods.name: Tramore
neighborhoods.description: Popular seaside town 11km south of Waterford city with a long sandy beach, amusement park, and family-friendly atmosphere
neighborhoods.character: Seaside suburban, beach lifestyle, family-oriented with growing year-round community

housing.predominantType: Semi-detached houses and modern estates, some coastal apartments

neighborhoods.character_notes: 3km Blue Flag beach. Tramore Racecourse. Good selection of pubs and restaurants. Surf school. 15-minute drive to Waterford city. Splashworld water park. Growing commuter town.

neighborhoods.name: Dunmore East
neighborhoods.description: Picturesque fishing village on the southeast coast with dramatic cliff walks, quiet coves, and a charming harbour
neighborhoods.character: Quiet coastal village, scenic, popular with artists and retirees, expat-friendly

housing.predominantType: Cottages, detached houses, some newer developments. Holiday lets convert to long-term in winter.

neighborhoods.character_notes: Popular with international visitors and retirees. Cliff walks to Counsellors Strand. Active fishing harbour. Seasonal farmers market. Limited services — 20-minute drive to Waterford city for shopping. Adventure centre and sailing club.

neighborhoods.name: Ferrybank
neighborhoods.description: Suburban area across the River Suir from Waterford city centre, straddling the Waterford-Kilkenny border with shopping centres and residential estates
neighborhoods.character: Suburban, convenient, good value housing with easy access to city centre

housing.predominantType: Modern housing estates, semi-detached and detached houses with gardens

neighborhoods.character_notes: Ferrybank Shopping Centre. Waterford Crystal visitor centre nearby. Bridge walk to city centre (10 min). Good schools. More space and value than city centre. Some areas technically in County Kilkenny.

neighborhoods.name: Dungarvan
neighborhoods.description: Attractive harbour town 30 minutes west of Waterford, known as a food destination with the Waterford Greenway cycling trail terminus
neighborhoods.character: Small-town charm, food culture, active lifestyle with Greenway, growing destination

housing.predominantType: Terraced and semi-detached houses in town, rural cottages and farmhouses in surrounding area

neighborhoods.character_notes: Waterford Greenway terminus (46km cycling/walking trail from Waterford city). West Waterford Festival of Food. Excellent restaurants including Tannery (Michelin-rated). Dungarvan Brewing Company. Harbour walks. Nearest town to Copper Coast UNESCO Global Geopark.
